title: myriophyllum aquaticum
intro: Myriophyllum aquaticum, also known as water velvet or water fern, is a semi-aquatic perennial plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Water velvet can be prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, and it is also used in herbal remedies in the form of capsules, tinctures, and salves.
